title: Prospective surveillance study of acute respiratory infections, influenza-like illness and seasonal influenza vaccine in a cohort of juvenile idiopathic arthritis patients.
ehr_evidence: The study was a prospective surveillance study in a tertiary Pediatric Rheumatology Clinic.
summary: Prospective surveillance study evaluating the frequency of acute respiratory infections (ARI) and influenza-like illness (ILI) in juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) patients, their impact on disease activity, and the immunogenicity/safety of the influenza vaccine.
key_findings: 20% of JIA flares were temporally associated with ARI
Influenza vaccine was immunogenic and safe in JIA patients
population: Juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) patients attending a tertiary Pediatric Rheumatology Clinic
